model_summary: Initial gVAR-ready observation model for recurrent nightmare-related awakenings with vivid nightmares, anticipatory sleep fear/avoidance, acute autonomic arousal on awakening, prolonged post-awakening activation, and difficulty resuming sleep. Design combines nightly morning diary (night-specific events and ratings), evening momentary EMA (pre-sleep anticipatory fear, bedtime routine adherence), and passive wearable HR/HRV continuous collection aggregated nightly. Predictors target modifiable behavioral and physiological levers: Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice, presleep wind-down adherence, paced breathing/grounding use, sleep-environment quality, wearable HRV arousal index, nonengagement with clock-checking, mindfulness/acceptance practice, and bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence. Sparse directed edges (P->C, P->P, C->C) select strongest incoming relations; dense plausibility matrices provided for full pairwise estimation. Total nodes: 14 (6 criteria + 8 predictors).
variable_selection_notes: Kept all six decomposed criteria from the complaint (C01–C06). Selected 8 high-signal, modifiable predictors prioritized from the provided hyde signals (IRT, routines, breathing/grounding, environment, HRV monitoring, clock-checking, mindfulness, settling routine). Avoided near-duplicate predictors (e.g., combined multiple 'winddown' elements into one presleep_winddown construct). Chosen measurement modes and sampling frequencies favor within-person fluctuation detection at nightly and pre-sleep timescales to support gVAR estimation.

time_blocks_local: daytime_random (09:00-20:00 window to sample baseline mood/arousal)
momentary_ema.notes: Evening pre-bed EMA anchors anticipatory fear, wind-down adherence, and bedside routine variables. One daytime random prompt improves variability estimates and helps deconfound diurnal effects.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary captures last-night nightmare occurrences, vividness, acute autonomic signs on awakening, duration to calm down, and difficulty resuming sleep. Use event-contingent follow-up prompts if awakening detected.

passive_streams.aggregation_strategy: nightly_aggregate (00:00-08:00 local) + event_flagging for transient awakenings
passive_streams.notes: Continuous heart rate and derived HRV (RMSSD) aggregated nightly (mean nocturnal HR, min HR, nocturnal RMSSD), with timestamped transient arousal peaks flagged for night-awakening windows.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: 28 days with 1 daily diary and 2 EMAs/day yields ~84 EMA readings + 28 diaries; passive nightly aggregates provide 28 additional observations per passive feature. Target >200 within-person observations per person for stable gVAR estimation; multi-subject pooled gVAR will improve inference.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 Likert scales for subjective ratings (increase variance). Passive HRV provides continuous variance. Event-contingent numeric durations (minutes) should be winsorized for outliers; consider log-transform for skewed duration variables.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Morning diary nonresponse likely if participant avoids mornings; include short push reminders. Passive stream dropouts expected with wearable nonwear — monitor and impute conservatively. Keep items short to preserve adherence.

safety_notes: This model is not a diagnostic tool. The complaint suggests significant nocturnal distress and physiologic arousal; if the participant reports severe daytime impairment, increasing panic, suicidal ideation, or risk behaviors, escalate to professional mental health services urgently. Provide crisis resources and clear instructions for seeking emergency care if acute risk emerges.

criteria_variables.label: Nightmare-triggered awakenings (occurrence)

measurement.item_or_signal: Last-night: Did you awaken due to a nightmare? (0=no, 1=yes) If yes: number_of_awakenings_last_night (integer) and primary_awakening_time (timestamp).
measurement.notes: Primary event flag is binary for occurrence; count helps examine frequency. For gVAR use binary+count or convert to intensity scale (0–3+).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: use count or binary as-is; consider person-mean centering and, for counts, log(1+count) if skewed

criteria_variables.label: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning report: On awakening from the nightmare, did you feel sweating/heart racing? Rate peak arousal 1-9. Passive: timestamped nocturnal HR spike (beats per minute) and HRV nadir (RMSSD ms) during flagged awakening.
measurement.notes: Combine subjective peak arousal rating with passive HR/HRV indicators. For gVAR, use subjective 1-9 as primary criterion node and passive HRV as predictor node.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: subjective rating person-mean centered; passive HR features standardized and person-centered

criteria_variables.label: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation

measurement.item_or_signal: How long did it take you to feel calm/settled after the awakening? (minutes) and subjective difficulty calming (1=very quick/easy to 9=very prolonged/difficult)
measurement.notes: Use both duration and Likert for redundancy; consider log transformation of duration for gVAR if heavily skewed.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log(1+minutes) for duration; person-mean centering for Likert

predictor_variables.label: Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice adherence
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: IRT targets nightmare content and reduces nightmare frequency/vividness by rehearsing alternative, non-threatening imagery.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Daily 5-10min imagery rehearsal session replacing nightmare script with practiced alternative imagery (non-pharmacological).

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you practice IRT last night or today? (1-9 adherence/intensity scale; 0=no practice event)
measurement.notes: Use intensity/adherence rating rather than binary to increase variance.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: IRT expected to reduce nightmare vividness over days to weeks.
IRT reduces recurrence of nightmare awakenings over days/weeks.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Avoid prompting imagery rehearsal if severe trauma without clinical support; include safety escalation wording.

This provides a precise operational construct aligned with the intended predictor (IRT adherence) and supports the mechanism of reducing nightmare distress through rehearsed imagery.

Measurement mode EMA_self_report via morning_diary aligns with within-person nightly fluctuations. Context: pseudoprofile mentions modifiable routines to support night awakenings; the leaf sits under night_awakenings_support in the BIO sleep_routines_and_practices branch.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence (settling routine) | Domain: BIOxPSYCHO | Mechanism: A structured settling routine after awakenings (neutral audio, non-engaging actions, low light path) reduces conditioned arousal and supports faster return to sleep. | Intervention examples: Pre-agreed neutral activities and settling scripts; avoid screens and clock-checking. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | If awakened: Did you use your return-to-bed settling routine? Rate adherence/effectiveness 1-9. | Prior ontology hint: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_routines_and_practices / night_awakenings_support / return_to_bed_settling_routine

edges.notes: Return-to-bed settling routine adherence expected to reduce likelihood of nightmare-triggered awakenings the same night or following.
IRT practice expected to reduce recurrence of nightmare awakenings over days to weeks.
Better sleep environment plausibly reduces awakenings; effect may be indirect.
Presleep wind-down reduces anticipatory fear immediately before bedtime.
Mindfulness/acceptance practice expected to lower anticipatory anxiety about sleep.
Passive HR/HRV is primarily an objective index of acute autonomic arousal on awakening (proxy).
Paced breathing/grounding expected to reduce duration of post-awakening activation.
Settling routine adherence strongly expected to shorten time to resume sleep.
Wind-down decreases baseline arousal and supports resuming sleep.

expected_collinearity_risks: P02 (presleep wind-down) and P08 (settling routine) may show moderate collinearity; monitor variance inflation and consider combining if necessary.
P07 (mindfulness) and P02 (wind-down) may correlate; center variables and inspect cross-correlations.
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: P04 (environment) may show low within-person variance (ceiling) if environment is stable; consider measuring discrete disturbances to increase variance.
P05 (wearable HRV) may have missingness (nonwear) leading to apparent floor effects; monitor wear compliance.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Use 1-9 Likert scales for subjective variables (already applied) to maximize within-person variance.
Capture counts/durations (minutes) for awakening-related constructs to add continuous variance.
Encourage daily short interventions (IRT, breathing) to increase variability in predictor adherence.
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: Pharmacotherapy and device-based somatic interventions were deprioritized (out of scope for non-pharmacological initial collection).
Many lower-ranked hyde leaf nodes (audiobook, aromatherapy) omitted to keep node count under cap.
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 14 nodes and a 28-day design (2 EMAs/day + daily diaries + nightly passive), the dataset will include sufficient repeated measures per node for within-person gVAR estimation if adherence is moderate. Passive HRV increases objective signal strength. Monitor missingness and address via within-person imputation and sensitivity analyses.

criteria.label: Nightmare-triggered awakenings (occurrence)
criteria.query_text_used: Nightmare-triggered awakenings (occurrence) | Operational item/signal: Last-night: Did you awaken due to a nightmare? (0=no, 1=yes) If yes: number_of_awakenings_last_night (integer) and primary_awakening_time (timestamp). | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Primary event flag is binary for occurrence; count helps examine frequency. For gVAR use binary+count or convert to intensity scale (0–3+).

mapping.rationale: Variable C01 corresponds to nightmares triggering awakenings; measurement is binary occurrence with nightly count from last night via EMA/self-report in a morning diary. The closest and most specific CRITERION leaf is nocturnal_awakenings_due_to_nightmares, which encodes awakenings caused by nightmares and aligns with binary occurrence and counting of awakenings.

measurement.item_or_signal: Last-night: Did you awaken due to a nightmare? (0=no, 1=yes) If yes: number_of_awakenings_last_night (integer) and primary_awakening_time (timestamp).
measurement.notes: Primary event flag is binary for occurrence; count helps examine frequency. For gVAR use binary+count or convert to intensity scale (0–3+).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: use count or binary as-is; consider person-mean centering and, for counts, log(1+count) if skewed

criteria.label: Nightmare vividness/intensity
criteria.query_text_used: Nightmare vividness/intensity | Operational item/signal: If awakened by nightmare: How vivid/real did the dream feel? (1=not vivid at all to 9=extremely vivid) | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Only asked when an awakening due to nightmare is reported; otherwise set to NA. High expected within-person variability.

mapping.rationale: Variable is a subjective, nightly self-report of nightmare vividness/intensity (EMA self-report via morning diary; item: 'How vivid/real did the dream feel?'). Among candidates, 25536 corresponds to nightmares_or_vivid_dreams, directly capturing vivid nightmares/dreams and aligns with the measurement context (EMA/self-report, morning diary) and the intended construct (nightmare vividness). This is the most specific operational leaf that matches the variable label and measurement item.

measurement.item_or_signal: If awakened by nightmare: How vivid/real did the dream feel? (1=not vivid at all to 9=extremely vivid)
measurement.notes: Only asked when an awakening due to nightmare is reported; otherwise set to NA. High expected within-person variability.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; keep ordinal as approx_continuous

criteria.label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed
criteria.query_text_used: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed | Operational item/signal: Right now (pre-bed): How afraid or worried are you about going to sleep because of nightmares? (1=not at all to 9=extremely) | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Measured each evening pre-bed to capture anticipatory fear; supports within-night lag analyses.

mapping.rationale: Mapping C03 to leaf pre_sleep_anxiety_and_rumination (idx 26298) because the variable captures anticipatory fear before sleep via an evening pre-bed EMA item (How afraid or worried are you about going to sleep because of nightmares?). It is a direct match to anticipatory sleep fear and fits the measurement mode (EMA_self_report, evening_prebed, since_last_prompt, momentary recall). This is the most specific operational construct among candidates that aligns with the described pseudoprofile's focus on pre-sleep anticipatory fear and modifiable sleep-related anxiety factors.

source_variable.label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed

measurement.item_or_signal: Right now (pre-bed): How afraid or worried are you about going to sleep because of nightmares? (1=not at all to 9=extremely)
measurement.recall_window: momentary (since last prompt, anchored to pre-bed)
measurement.notes: Measured each evening pre-bed to capture anticipatory fear; supports within-night lag analyses.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

criteria.label: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening
criteria.query_text_used: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening | Operational item/signal: Morning report: On awakening from the nightmare, did you feel sweating/heart racing? Rate peak arousal 1-9. Passive: timestamped nocturnal HR spike (beats per minute) and HRV nadir (RMSSD ms) during flagged awakening. | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Combine subjective peak arousal rating with passive HR/HRV indicators. For gVAR, use subjective 1-9 as primary criterion node and passive HRV as predictor node.

mapping.rationale: Best-match leaf: awakening_due_to_nightmare. Variable label Acute autonomic arousal on awakening aligns with nightmare-triggered awakening and measured autonomic arousal (subjective peak arousal rating 1-9 upon awakening; passive HR spike and HRV nadir during the flagged awakening). This leaf is the most specific criterion capturing post-awakening autonomic arousal linked to a nightmare within the DSM-5TR sleep-wake parasomnias domain.

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning report: On awakening from the nightmare, did you feel sweating/heart racing? Rate peak arousal 1-9. Passive: timestamped nocturnal HR spike (beats per minute) and HRV nadir (RMSSD ms) during flagged awakening.
measurement.notes: Combine subjective peak arousal rating with passive HR/HRV indicators. For gVAR, use subjective 1-9 as primary criterion node and passive HRV as predictor node.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: subjective rating person-mean centered; passive HR features standardized and person-centered

criteria.label: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation
criteria.query_text_used: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation | Operational item/signal: How long did it take you to feel calm/settled after the awakening? (minutes) and subjective difficulty calming (1=very quick/easy to 9=very prolonged/difficult) | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Use both duration and Likert for redundancy; consider log transformation of duration for gVAR if heavily skewed.

mapping.rationale: C05 maps to prolonged post-awakening activation and arousal. The most specific operational leaf is morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening (C05's post-awakening activation is typically captured as morning hyperarousal). The measurement is EMA_self_report via morning_diary with items about time to feel calm/settled after awakening and difficulty calming, which directly corresponds to the concept of post-awakening arousal requiring time to settle. This aligns with the stated goal of capturing post-awakening physiological/psychological activation and supports within-person fluctuation at nightly/morning times in the pseudoprofile.
mapping.leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / early-morning awakening insomnia / *morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening
mapping.leaf_full_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / early-morning awakening insomnia / morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening

measurement.item_or_signal: How long did it take you to feel calm/settled after the awakening? (minutes) and subjective difficulty calming (1=very quick/easy to 9=very prolonged/difficult)
measurement.notes: Use both duration and Likert for redundancy; consider log transformation of duration for gVAR if heavily skewed.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log(1+minutes) for duration; person-mean centering for Likert

criteria.label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings
criteria.query_text_used: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings | Operational item/signal: After the awakening, how difficult was it for you to fall back asleep? (1=not difficult to 9=extremely difficult) and estimated minutes to fall back asleep. | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Primary node is subjective difficulty 1-9; minutes secondary. Use person-centering.

mapping.rationale: C06 describes difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings. The measurement is EMA_self_report via a morning_diary item asking difficulty to fall back asleep after awakening, plus minutes to fall back asleep. The closest and most specific operational leaf is sleep_maintenance_difficulty (14901), representing post-awakening sleep maintenance difficulty. While nightmare-specific items exist (e.g., nocturnal awakenings due to nightmares), none precisely captures the post-awakening resumption difficulty as a separate construct; thus 14901 is the best-matching leaf.
mapping.leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / ICD-10 / diseases of the nervous system / episodic and paroxysmal disorders / sleep disorders / *sleep_maintenance_difficulty
mapping.leaf_full_path: CLINICAL / ICD-10 / diseases of the nervous system / episodic and paroxysmal disorders / sleep disorders / sleep_maintenance_difficulty

source_variable.label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings

measurement.item_or_signal: After the awakening, how difficult was it for you to fall back asleep? (1=not difficult to 9=extremely difficult) and estimated minutes to fall back asleep.
measurement.notes: Primary node is subjective difficulty 1-9; minutes secondary. Use person-centering.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log(1+minutes) if skewed; center Likert

predictors.label: Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice adherence
predictors.query_text_used: Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice adherence | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: IRT targets nightmare content and reduces nightmare frequency/vividness by rehearsing alternative, non-threatening imagery. | Intervention examples: Daily 5-10min imagery rehearsal session replacing nightmare script with practiced alternative imagery (non-pharmacological). | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Did you practice IRT last night or today? (1-9 adherence/intensity scale; 0=no practice event) | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / trauma_memory_imagery_and_narrative_work / imagery_and_rescripting_interventions / nightmare_imagery_rehearsal

mapping.rationale: Variable maps to Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice adherence. The measurement mode is EMA_self_report via morning_diary with item 'Did you practice IRT last night or today? (1-9 adherence/intensity scale; 0=no practice event)', signaling adherence to IRT practice. The most specific ontology leaf matching this intention is nightmare_imagery_rehearsal under PSYCHO / trauma_memory_imagery_and_narrative_work / imagery_and_rescripting_interventions, which directly reflects IRT practice for nightmare imagery. This provides a precise operational construct aligned with the intended predictor (IRT adherence) and supports the mechanism of reducing nightmare distress through rehearsed imagery.

source_variable.label: Imagery Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) practice adherence
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: IRT targets nightmare content and reduces nightmare frequency/vividness by rehearsing alternative, non-threatening imagery.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Daily 5-10min imagery rehearsal session replacing nightmare script with practiced alternative imagery (non-pharmacological).

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you practice IRT last night or today? (1-9 adherence/intensity scale; 0=no practice event)
measurement.notes: Use intensity/adherence rating rather than binary to increase variance.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: IRT expected to reduce nightmare vividness over days to weeks.
IRT reduces recurrence of nightmare awakenings over days/weeks.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Avoid prompting imagery rehearsal if severe trauma without clinical support; include safety escalation wording.

predictors.label: Presleep wind-down / detachment routine adherence
predictors.query_text_used: Presleep wind-down / detachment routine adherence | Domain: BIOxPSYCHO | Mechanism: Consistent wind-down routines decrease pre-sleep arousal and cognitive activation, reducing anticipatory fear and difficulty falling back asleep. | Intervention examples: 30–60min pre-bed low-stimulation routine (reading, warm shower, no screens). | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Did you follow your wind-down routine? Rate adherence 1-9. | Prior ontology hint: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_routines_and_practices / presleep_downshift / wind_down_routine

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf 6374: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_routines_and_practices / presleep_downshift / wind_down_routine. It most directly encodes presleep wind-down routine adherence as an operational sleep_routines_and_practices construct, matching the variable label. The mechanism rationale fits (wind-down routines reduce pre-sleep arousal/cognitive activation). EMA_self_report in the evening_prebed aligns with the intended timing for this predictor, and this leaf is more specific than broader or psychological detachment variants.

source_variable.label: Presleep wind-down / detachment routine adherence
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Consistent wind-down routines decrease pre-sleep arousal and cognitive activation, reducing anticipatory fear and difficulty falling back asleep.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: 30–60min pre-bed low-stimulation routine (reading, warm shower, no screens).

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you follow your wind-down routine? Rate adherence 1-9.
measurement.notes: Evening EMA captures immediate pre-sleep behaviors; complement with nightly diary adherence indicator.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Immediate reduction in anticipatory fear via pre-sleep downshift.

feasibility.notes: High feasibility; evening EMA alignment ensures coherence.

predictors.label: Paced breathing / grounding practice (post-awakening)
predictors.query_text_used: Paced breathing / grounding practice (post-awakening) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Brief paced breathing and grounding reduce autonomic arousal and speed recovery after awakening. | Intervention examples: 3-5 minute paced-breathing (4-6 breaths/min) or grounding exercise performed on awakening. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | If awakened: Did you use paced breathing/grounding? Rate effectiveness 1-9 and duration minutes. | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / breath_and_relaxation_skills / soothing_rhythm_breathing

mapping.rationale: Variable label 'Paced breathing / grounding practice (post-awakening)' matches the leaf 'paced_breathing_practice' under breath_and_relaxation_skills. The measurement item is event-contingent EMA/morning_diary asking if paced breathing/grounding was used and to rate effectiveness and duration, aligning exactly with the operational concept of paced_breathing_practice. Mechanism rationale (reducing autonomic arousal) fits the predictor purpose. Pseudoprofile context supports this as a modifiable post-awakening lever. Therefore this leaf is the most specific match.
mapping.leaf_full_path: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / stress_resilience_and_downregulation / physiological_downshift / breath_and_relaxation_skills / paced_breathing_practice

source_variable.label: Paced breathing / grounding practice (post-awakening)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Brief paced breathing and grounding reduce autonomic arousal and speed recovery after awakening.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: 3-5 minute paced-breathing (4-6 breaths/min) or grounding exercise performed on awakening.

measurement.item_or_signal: If awakened: Did you use paced breathing/grounding? Rate effectiveness 1-9 and duration minutes.
measurement.notes: Event-contingent; pair with passive HR change detection if possible.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: minutes log-transform if skewed; center Likert

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Ensure instructions avoid hyperventilation; recommend slow-guided protocols.

feasibility.notes: Event-dependent missingness possible if no awakening.

predictors.label: Sleep environment quality (comfort, light, temperature, noise)
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep environment quality (comfort, light, temperature, noise) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Higher sleep environment quality reduces nocturnal disturbances, supports return-to-sleep, and lowers baseline arousal. | Intervention examples: Adjust temperature, blackout curtains, low-light bathroom path, reduce noise; declutter bedroom. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Rate overall sleep environment quality last night (1=very poor to 9=excellent). Indicate notable disturbances (noise/light). | Prior ontology hint: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_environment / bedroom_physical_factors

mapping.rationale: The variable is Sleep environment quality measured as a self-reported rating last night. Among candidates, idx 4548 maps to self_assessment_and_logs / daily_and_retrospective_logs / sleep_quality_rating, which best aligns with a nightly, subjective, self-reported measure of sleep-related conditions. Although it specifies sleep quality rather than environment per se, sleep quality is strongly influenced by environment factors (comfort, light, temperature, noise). The measurement mode (EMA_self_report) and channel (morning_diary) are compatible in intent with the self-report/daily-log style, making this the most specific leaf that captures the intended predictive construct within the given options.

source_variable.label: Sleep environment quality (comfort, light, temperature, noise)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Higher sleep environment quality reduces nocturnal disturbances, supports return-to-sleep, and lowers baseline arousal.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Adjust temperature, blackout curtains, low-light bathroom path, reduce noise; declutter bedroom.

measurement.item_or_signal: Rate overall sleep environment quality last night (1=very poor to 9=excellent). Indicate notable disturbances (noise/light).
measurement.notes: Relatively stable but can vary night-to-night (guests, windows open).

expected_effects.rationale: Better environment supports faster return to sleep.

feasibility.notes: Self-report feasible but low within-person variance may reduce predictive power.

predictors.label: Nightly wearable HR/HRV arousal index (passive)
predictors.query_text_used: Nightly wearable HR/HRV arousal index (passive) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Objective autonomic arousal markers (nocturnal HR spikes, RMSSD reductions) index physiological activation during nightmares and recovery. | Intervention examples: Provide biofeedback/training but measurement-only node is passive. | Measurement: wearable_sensor | Nightly mean nocturnal HR, nocturnal RMSSD (ms), count of HR spikes above threshold within night-awakening windows; aggregated 00:00-08:00 | Prior ontology hint: BIO / personalization_monitoring_and_data_feedback / wearables_and_home_monitoring

mapping.rationale: The variable describes a Nightly wearable HR/HRV arousal index measured passively via a wearable. Among candidates, idx=4623 corresponds to photoplethysmography_hrv_wearable, the most specific leaf capturing HRV metrics derived from wearable photoplethysmography (e.g., RMSSD) and heart-rate signals, aligned with autonomic arousal during nocturnal awakenings. This leaf sits under HRV and autonomic/cardiovascular physiology in wearables, matching the measurement mode (wearable_sensor) and passive daily aggregation (nightly 00:00–08:00) implied by the variable.

source_variable.label: Nightly wearable HR/HRV arousal index (passive)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Objective autonomic arousal markers (nocturnal HR spikes, RMSSD reductions) index physiological activation during nightmares and recovery.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Provide biofeedback/training but measurement-only node is passive.

measurement.item_or_signal: Nightly mean nocturnal HR, nocturnal RMSSD (ms), count of HR spikes above threshold within night-awakening windows; aggregated 00:00-08:00
measurement.notes: Passive continuous stream offers objective complement to subjective arousal; requires consistent wear.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: standardize nightly values per person; consider smoothing short gaps

expected_effects.rationale: HR spikes temporally aligned with subjective acute arousal on awakening.

source_variable.risk_ethics_notes: Privacy: ensure secure handling of wearable data.

feasibility.notes: Requires participant wearable and compliance; passive data rich for gVAR.

predictors.label: Nonengagement with clock-checking (nighttime checking behavior)
predictors.query_text_used: Nonengagement with clock-checking (nighttime checking behavior) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Checking the clock can maintain arousal and frustrate return to sleep; nonengagement reduces conditioned arousal. | Intervention examples: Place clock out of view or use nonengagement strategy on awakening. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Did you check the time/clock during the night or after awakening? (count or yes/no + impact rating 1-9) | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / sleep_related_cognitive_behavioral_interventions / nonengagement_with_clock_checking

mapping.rationale: The variable label is Nonengagement with clock-checking (nighttime checking behavior) and the candidate leaf 6341 exactly corresponds to nonengagement_with_clock_checking (night_awakenings_support). This makes it the most specific operational construct among the candidates for reducing clock-checking-related arousal, which aligns with the mechanism that clock checking maintains arousal and impedes return to sleep. Although the leaf is categorized under BIO in the ontology, its label precisely matches the intended predictor, providing the closest semantic and operational mapping to the PSYCHO conceptual target. The measurement for P06 is EMA_self_report/morning_diary; the nonengagement_with_clock_checking leaf represents the same behavioral target within a sleep routines/practices context, making it the best single match.

source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Place clock out of view or use nonengagement strategy on awakening.

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you check the time/clock during the night or after awakening? (count or yes/no + impact rating 1-9)
measurement.notes: Count provides richer info but may be noisy; use binary+impact rating for simplicity.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: use binary or small-count coding; center

expected_effects.rationale: Clock-checking likely increases time to resume sleep.

predictors.label: Mindfulness/acceptance practice (in-bed or pre-sleep)
predictors.query_text_used: Mindfulness/acceptance practice (in-bed or pre-sleep) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Mindfulness and acceptance reduce anticipatory worry and support faster emotional downregulation after arousing events. | Intervention examples: Short body-scan, labeling thoughts practice at bedtime or on awakening. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Did you use a mindfulness/acceptance practice pre-bed or after awakening? Rate helpfulness 1-9. | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_based_interventions / mindful_labeling_of_thoughts

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf corresponds to a concrete body-based mindfulness practice suitable for in-bed/pre-sleep contexts. Mindfulness body scan is a common presleep wind-down technique; the measurement item asks about use of a mindfulness/acceptance practice pre-bed or after awakening and rates helpfulness, which aligns with body_scan_practice in the mindfulness domain. Mechanism: promotes downregulation of autonomic arousal and reduces anticipatory worry, supporting faster emotional regulation at night. Pseudoprofile context uses evening_prebed + morning_diary sampling; fits within-nightly measurement for gVAR estimation.

source_variable.label: Mindfulness/acceptance practice (in-bed or pre-sleep)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness and acceptance reduce anticipatory worry and support faster emotional downregulation after arousing events.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Short body-scan, labeling thoughts practice at bedtime or on awakening.

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you use a mindfulness/acceptance practice pre-bed or after awakening? Rate helpfulness 1-9.
measurement.notes: Measure both pre-sleep and post-awakening usage if possible; combine into single daily intensity score.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: center

feasibility.notes: Practices must be brief to ensure adherence.

predictors.label: Bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence (settling routine)
predictors.query_text_used: Bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence (settling routine) | Domain: BIOxPSYCHO | Mechanism: A structured settling routine after awakenings (neutral audio, non-engaging actions, low light path) reduces conditioned arousal and supports faster return to sleep. | Intervention examples: Pre-agreed neutral activities and settling scripts; avoid screens and clock-checking. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | If awakened: Did you use your return-to-bed settling routine? Rate adherence/effectiveness 1-9. | Prior ontology hint: BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_routines_and_practices / night_awakenings_support / return_to_bed_settling_routine

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf: night_awakenings_support / return_to_bed_settling_routine. Variable label Bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence maps directly to the measurement target: 'return_to_bed_settling_routine' as a predictor of post-awakening sleep continuity. The measurement item (If awakened: Did you use your return-to-bed settling routine? 1-9) aligns with this leaf's scope. Mechanism: settling routine after awakenings reduces conditioned arousal and promotes rapid re-entry to sleep. Measurement mode EMA_self_report via morning_diary aligns with within-person nightly fluctuations. Context: pseudoprofile mentions modifiable routines to support night awakenings; the leaf sits under night_awakenings_support in the BIO sleep_routines_and_practices branch.

source_variable.label: Bedtime/return-to-bed settling routine adherence (settling routine)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: A structured settling routine after awakenings (neutral audio, non-engaging actions, low light path) reduces conditioned arousal and supports faster return to sleep.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Pre-agreed neutral activities and settling scripts; avoid screens and clock-checking.

measurement.item_or_signal: If awakened: Did you use your return-to-bed settling routine? Rate adherence/effectiveness 1-9.
measurement.notes: Key behavior to measure because of strong hyde signal for return-to-bed practices.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: center

expected_effects.rationale: Settling routine directly reduces difficulty resuming sleep.

feasibility.notes: Simple nightly self-report tied to awakenings.
